introduction: In Telangana, a Marriage Certificate serves as official documentation of a married couple’s relationship. It is regulated by the Registration and Stamps Department and can be applied for via TS MeeSeva.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Processing Time: Issued generally on the same day as the physical verification visit.
  • Application Fee: Around ₹100-₹200 including specific MeeSeva service and statutory charges.
  • A Marriage Certificate is valid for a lifetime.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Marriage Registration

Note: You need to provide documents for Proof of Age, Proof of Address, Marriage Proof, and Witnesses.
Proof of Identity & Age
  • Birth Certificate
  • SSC memos
  • Passport
Proof of Address
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Passport
  • Registered Lease Agreement
Evidence of Marriage & Affidavit
  • Actual Wedding ceremony photos
  • Marriage invitation card and wedding hall receipt
  • Joint affidavit swearing facts of marriage
Witnesses
  • Three adult witnesses
  • Witness identification (Aadhaar)
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via TS MeeSeva / IGRS Portal

Telangana offers online marriage registration through the TS MeeSeva portal and the IGRS (Inspector General of Registration and Stamps) portal, enabling slot booking and document upload from home.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit TS MeeSeva or IGRS Portal
    Go to ts.meeseva.telangana.gov.in or registration.telangana.gov.in.
  2. Register / Login
    Click "Sign Up". Enter your mobile number and email ID, verify via OTP. Log in with your credentials.
  3. Select Marriage Registration Service
    Navigate to "Registration & Stamps" → "Marriage Registration". Choose the applicable Act (Hindu Marriage Act / Special Marriage Act).
  4. Fill Application Form
    Enter: full names (as per Aadhaar/SSC), date and place of marriage, mandal, district, religion, details of 3 witnesses (name, address, Aadhaar number).
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies (JPG/PDF): Birth Certificate/SSC marks memo, Aadhaar, address proof, ceremony photos, wedding invitation / hall receipt, and 3 witness ID proofs.
  6. Pay Fee & Book SRO Slot
    Pay ₹100–₹200 online via the integrated payment gateway. Book a slot at your jurisdictional Sub-Registrar's Office (SRO). Download the Acknowledgement Slip.
  7. Attend SRO on Booked Date
    Both spouses and all 3 witnesses must appear in person. Biometrics (fingerprints/photos) are captured. All parties sign the marriage register. Certificate is issued same day.
Download Certificate: After SRO registration, download the digitally signed certificate from registration.telangana.gov.in using your application number.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at MeeSeva Center or Sub-Registrar Office

Visit your nearest MeeSeva Center or the jurisdictional Sub-Registrar Office (SRO) of the mandal where the marriage was solemnized. Working hours: Monday – Saturday, 10:30 AM – 5:00 PM.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Locate the Correct SRO / MeeSeva Center
    Find your jurisdictional SRO via registration.telangana.gov.in. Alternatively, visit any MeeSeva center for operator-assisted filing.
  2. Obtain the Marriage Memorandum Form
    Collect the Memorandum of Marriage form from the SRO counter or MeeSeva center. Available in Telugu and English.
  3. Fill All Details
    Enter: both spouses' full names (as per SSC/Aadhaar), marriage date and place, mandal, district, religion, and details of 3 witnesses (name, address, Aadhaar).
  4. Prepare a Notarized Joint Affidavit
    Both spouses must swear a joint affidavit before a Notary or First Class Magistrate confirming the marriage date, place, marital status at the time of marriage, and religion. Affidavit must be on ₹100 stamp paper.
  5. Attach Self-Attested Copies
    Attach: SSC marks memo / Birth Certificate (age proof), Aadhaar (address proof), ceremony photos, wedding invitation / hall receipt, and Aadhaar / Voter ID proofs of 3 witnesses. Bring all originals.
  6. Submit & Pay Fee
    Submit the complete form at the counter. Pay ₹100–₹200 in cash. Collect the Acknowledgement Receipt.
  7. Attend Verification & Collect Certificate
    Both spouses and all 3 witnesses appear before the Sub-Registrar. Biometrics are captured; all sign the marriage register. Certificate is issued same day or downloadable from IGRS Telangana.
Helpline: Telangana Registration Dept: 040-23300615. For late registration (after 1 year) approval of the District Registrar is required.
afterApplying:
After Registration - What Next?

Verification Required

Physical verification of the husband, wife, and witnesses remains compulsory. Verification confirms document authenticity.

Collect Certificate

Upon concluding signatures at the Sub-Registrar's office, the marriage certificate secures entry into the permanent register and a copy is furnished to you.
